perturbation.original_text: (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX within seven (7) days after termination of the contract.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause about a written request from NYNEX to return Confidential Information and replacing it with a return upon contract termination creates a contradiction. NYNEX could request specific confidential information be returned during the contract according to the original language, however, now all Confidential Information is only due upon termination. This introduces ambiguity as the rights of NYNEX to regain possession of confidential information during the term of the contract are now unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX within seven (7) days after termination of the contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows NYNEX to request Confidential Information be returned at any time during the contract with a written request. The changed text removes this ability, only requiring return upon termination. The contradicted text section includes the changed text, which now states that Confidential Information should be returned within seven days of contract termination. This contradicts the original intent of NYNEX being able to request and receive confidential information during the term of the contract, as now the consultant is only obliged to return it upon termination. This creates ambiguity and introduces a contradiction as NYNEX's rights to regain possession of confidential information during the term are unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 6. CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees to confidentiality obligations. CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss arising out of any failure of CONSULTANT or its employees to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the part about agents and subcontractors being bound by similar non-disclosure agreements, a contradiction arises. The removal introduces ambiguity concerning the protection of Confidential Information by subcontractors and agents. This is an in-text contradiction as the original section mandated that the Consultant's agents and subcontractors also abide by the Non-Disclosure Agreement, however, since they are not referenced here, it is unclear. This creates contradiction when the section later says CONSULTANT agrees to defend NYNEX from an failure of the CONSULTANT 'or it's employees' without referencing agents or subcontractors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees to confidentiality obligations. CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss arising out of any failure of CONSULTANT or its employees to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit obligation for the CONSULTANT to ensure that agents and subcontractors are bound by confidentiality agreements. This creates a contradiction because while the original text explicitly included agents and subcontractors, the changed text only mentions 'employees'. The contradicted text repeats this lack of mentioning agents and subcontractors. This is a strong contradiction because it creates uncertainty about whether the CONSULTANT is responsible for breaches of confidentiality by agents and subcontractors, potentially conflicting with the original intent of the agreement to protect confidential information across all parties working with the CONSULTANT.

perturbation.original_text: 7. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second sentence regarding survival of obligations, it is now ambiguous as to what happens to the confidential information after the contract is terminated. Section 2(e) indicates that the Confidential Information will be returned to NYNEX after termination. The omission of the sentence in section 7 contradicts section 2(e), as there is no enforcement language that indicates the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX within seven (7) days after termination of the contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the obligations regarding confidential information survive termination of the agreement. The changed text removes this clause. Section 2(e) of the contract states that the confidential information must be returned to NYNEX within seven days after termination. Without the survival clause, it is unclear whether this obligation still exists after termination, thus creating a direct contradiction. The omission breaks the clear enforceability of section 2(e), thus creating a contradiction.
